title: RESOLVED: runlease-phaseobserver concurrency-hygiene (all 3 sub-fixes landed)
summary: TWO of three sub-fixes LANDED. (1) runlease seams: 2675394a (per-instance writer struct; seam tests t.Parallel; isolation test; -race -count=5 -parallel=8 green). (2) phaseobserver PRODUCTION hygiene: d413e5ec — dead o.mu removed (caller-trace PROVED Run is single-goroutine: package-level Run constructs a LOCAL *Observer, never shared; both reviewers independently re-verified incl. the adapters/observer.Observer being a distinct type) + per-iteration make(chan time.Time) killed via a hoisted nil-channel stopChan helper (AllocsPerRun==0 test). REMAINING (this item, low): the 5 bare time.Sleep(40-150ms) test barriers (apicover_named_test.go:37; coverage_test.go:49,272; phaseobserver_test.go:75,286) — a flake source under CI contention, not a production bug.
root_cause: Test barriers grafted onto wall-clock sleeps instead of deterministic signals (the production single-threaded-but-mutexed + per-iter-alloc issues are now fixed).
fix: Replace each of the 5 time.Sleep barriers with a deterministic signal: for observer-writes-a-file waits, poll-until-file-nonempty with a bounded deadline (or an injected Ready channel); for the StopAfterMS-driven waits, drive via the existing StopAfterMS/injected-clock seam and await the observer's own completion signal instead of a fixed sleep. Keep each test's intent; prove stability under -race -count=5 -parallel=8.
acceptance: all 5 sleep barriers replaced by deterministic signals; suite stable under -race -count=5 -parallel=8
connects_to: observer-sink-close-race (adapters/observer sibling — verify before removing the mutex)
operator_note: RESOLVED 2026-07-14 (operator, fable+tdd, reviewers Approve, CI green). All 3 sub-fixes landed: (1) runlease per-instance write seams 2675394a; (2) phaseobserver production hygiene d413e5ec (dead-mutex removal proven single-goroutine + per-iter chan alloc kill); (3) phaseobserver test-sleep determinism <pending-commit> — all 5 time.Sleep barriers removed: Pattern A tests use <-done as the barrier (already-closed shutdown fires on first select iter), Pattern B tests use synchronous StopAfterMS-driven termination; suite stable -race -count=5 -parallel=8, 0 sleeps remain. Weight ->0.05 (done).
